author: Sofia Martínez
title: AI Chatbots on Your Website: The Feature That's Closing Deals at 2 AM
description: 60% of service inquiries happen outside business hours. An AI chatbot on your website captures those leads before they go to a competitor.

sections.p: The most expensive thing about your website isn't what you paid to build it — it's the leads it loses when no one is available to respond. Website visitor data consistently shows that 55–65% of contact intent happens outside 9-to-5 business hours. An AI chatbot captures that intent, qualifies the lead, and books or collects contact information before the visitor leaves.

sections.h2: What a well-configured AI chatbot does differently

sections.p: A generic chatbot asks 'how can I help?' and sends a form. A well-configured AI chatbot knows your services, can answer pricing questions, qualifies the lead based on what they need, and either books a time or collects the contact information needed for a human follow-up — all without anyone being awake.

The key is the initial prompt and training. We configure chatbots with the complete service menu, pricing ranges, common objections, and qualification questions for each client's business. The chatbot doesn't just capture leads — it delivers pre-qualified, context-rich leads to the sales inbox.

sections.h2: The impact on lead response time

list: Without chatbot: average lead response time 4–6 hours (or next morning for after-hours inquiries).
With chatbot: immediate response 24/7, with full qualification and booking link delivered in under 2 minutes.
Conversion rate impact: companies that respond within 5 minutes convert leads at 9x the rate of companies that respond in an hour.

sections.h2: Chatbot use cases by business type

sections.p: Service businesses use chatbots for appointment booking and service qualification. E-commerce uses them for product recommendations and order status. Professional services use them for initial intake and consultation scheduling. The configuration is different for each, but the core outcome is the same: no more leads lost to slow response.

A chatbot that doesn't feed your CRM is only doing half its job. Every conversation should create or update a contact record, log the conversation, and trigger the appropriate follow-up sequence. When the chatbot conversation is in the CRM, your team can pick up exactly where the bot left off — no 'how can I help you today' to a lead who already explained their situation.

sections.quote: Your best salesperson works 24 hours a day, never takes vacation, and responds in seconds. It's your website chatbot. Most businesses haven't hired it yet.
